originalTitle: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
summary: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health, as previously announced, and many of the responses we've seen so far are full of confusion, frustration, […]
content: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we’ve spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health, as previously announced, and many of the responses we’ve seen so far are full of confusion, frustration, and requests to get the old app back. One post on Reddit calls out a common issue, saying, “I can’t even completely fill up my home screen. They only have 2 large tiles available and I can’t just scroll down to see everything.” The landing page has a small section up top showing steps and some other basic stats, but part of the app’s main page is now reserved for recent activity updates and chatty notes from Google’s AI health coach. [Media: https://www.reddit.com/r/fitbit/comments/1tjddsd/fitbit_coach_just_sucks/] The AI didn’t have much to say to me, but for my senior editor, Richard Lawler, it started a conversation about today’s plans that he wasn’t quite ready to have with a chatbot. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Screenshot_20260526-132529.png?quality=90&strip=all] Not everyone is annoyed by the AI bot however, with one person commenting, “When I ask it to design a moderate workout using my office gym equipment, circuit style, I usually end up feeling great afterwards.” Another person called it “quite a helpful feature,” showing how they were able to update their sleep log with a missed session by chatting with the AI bot. Another user said, “This graphic UI looks like something an 8 year old would make,” while someone else complained, “Why must I now scroll through paragraphs of AI slop on every tab before I can actually see my activities and data? I don’t want or need to read platitudes about my 15 minute walk to the grocery store. I want to see my stats from my morning run.”  One post on Google’s help center sums things up, saying, “This app is a huge disappointment and a total time drain to get minimal results. How can I get back to using what worked?!” Many others  were in agreement, with one reply saying, “it’s no longer a genuine fitness app.”  On Google’s blog post, its sample image shows a version of the Today screen with all of the information and an AI chat that we couldn’t get to show up, but did appear for some users. There doesn’t seem to be any way to remove the Ask Coach / activity window that takes up so much of the screen, but the bot can be disabled from within the new app’s Feature Privacy Controls. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/sb-google-health-screenshot-1.png?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/sb-google-health-screenshot-3.png?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/sb-google-heaelth-screenshot-2.png?quality=90&strip=all] Even though I knew the switch to Google Health was coming, I was still disoriented for the first several minutes after opening the app this morning.  If you want to see more of your stats and health tracking data, you have to either swipe left in the small top box on the “Today” page or tab over to the “Health” page. To find logs for my rowing workouts that I had stored in the old Fitbit app, I had to go into “Health,” then down to the “Fitness” section in “Focus areas”, where my logs were viewable under “Exercise days.” In the old Fitbit app, I could see the “Exercise days” block by just scrolling down on the app’s main “Today” page.  According to a support page, if you have a supported wearable connected, Google Health shows two additional tabs for Fitness and Sleep that would make things easier, but before the redesign I didn’t need those. While Google’s Rishi Chandra told The Verge earlier this month that Google Health will eventually support third-party wearables, my Nothing Watch Pro 3 currently isn’t enough to unlock those two extra tabs.
chineseTitle: Google Health 已上线，但很多人希望恢复 Fitbit 应用程序
chineseSummary: Fitbit 应用程序已不复存在。随着新款 Fitbit Air 的推出（一旦我们花更多时间使用它，您可以期待对其进行全面评测），正如之前宣布的那样，Google 已正式将其替换为 Google Health，到目前为止，我们看到的许多反应都充满了困惑、沮丧，[...]
chineseContent: Fitbit 应用程序已不复存在。随着新款 Fitbit Air 的推出（一旦我们花更多时间使用它，您可以期待对其进行全面评测），正如之前宣布的那样，Google 已正式将其替换为 Google Health，到目前为止，我们看到的许多回应都充满了困惑、沮丧，并要求恢复旧应用程序。 Reddit 上的一篇帖子指出了一个常见问题，称“我什至无法完全填满主屏幕。他们只有 2 个大块可用，我无法向下滚动查看所有内容。”登陆页面顶部有一小部分显示步数和其他一些基本统计数据，但应用程序主页的一部分现在保留用于最近的活动更新和谷歌人工智能健康教练的闲聊笔记。 [媒体：https://www.reddit.com/r/fitbit/comments/1tjddsd/fitbit_coach_just_sucks/] 人工智能对我来说没什么可说的，但对于我的高级编辑理查德·劳勒来说，它开始了一场关于今天计划的对话，而他还没有准备好与聊天机器人进行对话。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Screenshot_20260526-132529.png?quality=90&strip=all] 然而，并不是每个人都对人工智能机器人感到恼火，有人评论道，“当我要求它使用我的办公室健身器材、循环式设计一项适度的锻炼时，我通常最终会感觉很棒。”另一个人称其为“非常有用的功能”，展示了他们如何通过与人工智能机器人聊天来更新错过的睡眠日志。另一位用户说，“这个图形 UI 看起来就像 8 岁孩子会做的东西”，而其他人则抱怨道，“为什么我现在必须滚动浏览每个选项卡上的人工智能段落，然后才能真正看到我的活动和数据？我不想也不需要阅读关于我步行 15 分钟到农场的陈词滥调。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
